checks.finding: design.md defines the shell, environment, quoted Python verifier, consent decision, bundle writer, and REQ/AC links.
The design specifies a one-way input-to-verifier-to-consent flow with no declared circular dependencies.
The data plan and security specification define process-local HMAC inputs, fixture lifecycle, and no persisted-data change.
The internal token, canonical-message, exit-code, consent-label, and output-bundle contract is preserved.
The security specification covers injection prevention, real HMAC validation, fail-closed consent, key protection, and planned verification.
The frontend specification correctly marks browser work out of scope and agrees with the local CLI implementation.
TEST-001 through TEST-007 cover shell and PowerShell real-HMAC, tamper, hostile-input, invalid-validation, and isolated-fixture paths.
Every planned script, helper, decision point, and test participant is defined and scoped.
No public contract, architecture, or data-model change requires an ADR for this internal correction.
No rendered UI exists, so visual design-system requirements are inapplicable.
The plan preserves existing SDD_SUDO semantics and introduces no domain model, persistence, or external contract.
